revision.comment: [[壱岐古墳群]]から転記。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 笹塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|250px]] |所属 = |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町百合畑触936 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|48|10.25|N|129|42|14.75|E|region:JP-42_type:landmark|display=inline|name=笹塚古墳}} |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径約66m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]]（3室）
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]） |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末頃
（[[7世紀]]末頃まで追葬） |被葬者 = |出土品 = 金銅亀形飾金具1点・銀製品1点・金銅製品39点など多数 |史跡 = |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = }} '''笹塚古墳'''（ささづかこふん）は、壱岐市勝本町百合畑触にある古墳。形状は[[円墳]]。 6世紀後半から末頃の築造で、追葬は7世紀末頃までと推定される{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 [[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1990年]]（平成2年）に旧勝本町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされ、[[2002年]]-[[2003年]]（平成14年-15年）にも調査がなされている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 笹塚古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。墳丘は円形で2段築成であり、直径約66メートルを測る。 === 埋葬施設 === 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『壱岐国風土記』には石室内に文字が存在する旨の記載が見えるが、昭和初期の段階で文字は失われている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 :* 石室全長：14.8メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}（15.2メートル） :* 玄室：長さ3.8メートル、幅2.7メートル、高さ3.5メートル :* 中室：長さ4.5メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.8メートル :* 前室：長さ4.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル :* 羨道：長さ2.3メートル 玄室最奥部には凝灰岩製の組合式[[箱式石棺]]が据えられ、その規模は長さ2メートル、幅1メートル、深さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。かつては手前に石棺もう1基が存在したというが、その痕跡は見つかっていない{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 === 出土品 === 古くより盗掘を受けているが、出土品として国内唯一の出土例である亀形辻金具をはじめ金銅装馬具・鉄製杏葉等があり、複数馬具の副葬や高い細工技術などの特徴を有している{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。そのほか須恵器・土師器坏・朝鮮半島系陶質土器・鉄製品・ガラス製小玉・金環・スプーン状銅製品といった多数の遺物も見つかっている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらの遺物は「長崎県笹塚古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。

revision.comment: 体裁修正。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 笹塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町百合畑触936 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 10.25 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 14.75 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径約66m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]） |出土品 = 金銅亀形飾金具・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 笹塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''笹塚古墳'''（ささづかこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町百合畑触にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1990年]]（平成2年）に旧勝本町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされ、[[2002年]]-[[2003年]]（平成14年-15年）にも調査がなされている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 笹塚古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。墳丘は円形で2段築成であり、直径約66メートルを測る。 築造時期は6世紀後半から末頃で、追葬は7世紀末頃までと推定される{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 埋葬施設 == 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『壱岐国風土記』には石室内に文字が存在する旨の記載が見えるが、昭和初期の段階で文字は失われている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：14.8メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}（15.2メートル） * 玄室：長さ3.8メートル、幅2.7メートル、高さ3.5メートル * 中室：長さ4.5メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 前室：長さ4.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 羨道：長さ2.3メートル 玄室最奥部には凝灰岩製の組合式[[箱式石棺]]が据えられ、その規模は長さ2メートル、幅1メートル、深さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。かつては手前に石棺もう1基が存在したというが、その痕跡は見つかっていない{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 出土品 == 古くより盗掘を受けているが、出土品として国内唯一の出土例である亀形辻金具をはじめ金銅装馬具・鉄製杏葉等があり、複数馬具の副葬や高い細工技術などの特徴を有している{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。そのほか須恵器・土師器坏・朝鮮半島系陶質土器・鉄製品・ガラス製小玉・金環・スプーン状銅製品といった多数の遺物も見つかっている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらの遺物は「長崎県笹塚古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県笹塚古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2007年（平成19年）6月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011271|長崎県笹塚古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 長崎県笹塚古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金銅亀形飾金具 1点 ** 銀製品 1点 ** 金銅製品 39点 ** 青銅製品 4点 ** 鉄製品 52点 ** ガラス製品 46点 ** 土器 19点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 笹塚古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=笹塚古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|笹塚古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=笹塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=笹塚古墳 -市内遺跡発掘調査事業に伴う発掘調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第5集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:ささつかこふん いきし}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 笹塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] |画像キャプション = 墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = [[壱岐古墳群]] |所在地 = [[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町百合畑触936 |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 48|緯度秒 = 10.25 |経度度 = 129|経度分 = 42|経度秒 = 14.75 |ISO = JP-42 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径約66m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]]
（内部に組合式[[箱式石棺]]） |出土品 = 金銅亀形飾金具・銀製品・金銅製品ほか副葬品多数 |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半-末 |被葬者 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「壱岐古墳群」に包含 |有形文化財 = 出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nagasaki |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 笹塚古墳 |ラベル位置 = bottom }} '''笹塚古墳'''（ささづかこふん）は、[[長崎県]][[壱岐市]]勝本町百合畑触にある[[古墳]]。 形状は[[円墳]]。[[壱岐古墳群]]（国の[[史跡]]）を構成する古墳の1つ。出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 概要 == 壱岐島中央部の丘陵上に築造された古墳である。[[1989年]]（平成元年）に長崎県教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査が、[[1990年]]（平成2年）に旧勝本町教育委員会による墳丘・石室の実測調査がなされ、[[2002年]]-[[2003年]]（平成14年-15年）にも調査がなされている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 笹塚古墳]（長崎県教育庁学芸文化課「長崎県の遺跡大辞典」）。。墳丘は円形で2段築成であり、直径約66メートルを測る。 築造時期は6世紀後半から末頃で、追葬は7世紀末頃までと推定される{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設は複室構造の横穴式石室で、玄室・中室・前室の3室と羨道で構成され、南西方に開口する{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。江戸時代にはすでに石室は開口し、『壱岐国風土記』には石室内に文字が存在する旨の記載が見えるが、昭和初期の段階で文字は失われている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：14.8メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}（15.2メートル） * 玄室：長さ3.8メートル、幅2.7メートル、高さ3.5メートル * 中室：長さ4.5メートル、幅2メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 前室：長さ4.2メートル、幅1.8メートル、高さ1.8メートル * 羨道：長さ2.3メートル 玄室最奥部には凝灰岩製の組合式[[箱式石棺]]が据えられ、その規模は長さ2メートル、幅1メートル、深さ0.7メートル{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。かつては手前に石棺もう1基が存在したというが、その痕跡は見つかっていない{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 == 出土品 == 古くより盗掘を受けているが、出土品として国内唯一の出土例である亀形辻金具をはじめ金銅装馬具・鉄製杏葉等があり、複数馬具の副葬や高い細工技術などの特徴を有している{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。そのほか須恵器・土師器坏・朝鮮半島系陶質土器・鉄製品・ガラス製小玉・金環・スプーン状銅製品といった多数の遺物も見つかっている{{Sfn|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。これらの遺物は「長崎県笹塚古墳出土品」として国の重要文化財に指定されている。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 長崎県笹塚古墳出土品（考古資料） - 明細は以下。2007年（平成19年）6月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011271|長崎県笹塚古墳出土品}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 長崎県笹塚古墳出土品]（長崎県ホームページ）。。 ** 金銅亀形飾金具 1点 ** 銀製品 1点 ** 金銅製品 39点 ** 青銅製品 4点 ** 鉄製品 52点 ** ガラス製品 46点 ** 土器 19点 == 関連施設 == * [[壱岐市立一支国博物館]]（壱岐市芦辺町深江鶴亀触） - 笹塚古墳の出土品を保管。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板 * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2017|chapter=|title=壱岐市の文化財|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|壱岐市の文化財|2017}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2001|chapter=笹塚古墳|title=長崎県の地名|volume=[[日本歴史地名大系]]43|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490433|ref={{Harvid|笹塚古墳（平凡社）|2001}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=田川肇|year=2002|chapter=笹塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490105991|ref={{Harvid|笹塚古墳（続古墳）|2002}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/14748 |title=県内古墳詳細分布調査報告書 -県内古墳の墳丘・石室の資料化に伴う報告書-|volume=長崎県文化財調査報告書第106集|publisher=長崎県教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=|title=笹塚古墳 -市内遺跡発掘調査事業に伴う発掘調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第5集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2012|chapter=|title=壱岐の島の古墳群 -現状調査-|volume=壱岐市文化財調査報告書第20集|publisher=壱岐市教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 壱岐古墳群] - 長崎県ホームページ「長崎県の文化財」 {{デフォルトソート:ささつかこふん いきし}} [[Category:壱岐古墳群]] [[Category:長崎県の古墳]] [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:長崎県の重要文化財]] [[Category:長崎県にある国指定の史跡]]

[[Category:bump 1-20]]

{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]]

It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]

The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]]

It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.comment: linking [[Round Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]]

Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>.
