revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ... 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]]

The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ... 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''掛木古墳'''}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ... 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki[[Category:nowiki former opening]]&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki[[Category:nowiki former opening]]&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki[[Category:nowiki former opening]]&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; {{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki[[Category:nowiki former opening]]&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki[[Category:nowiki former opening]]&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]], {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|[[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]], {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...[[Category:nowiki former closing]] 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''[[Category:nowiki former closing]]掛木古墳'''[[Category:nowiki former closing]]}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ([[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]]. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:横穴式石室]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:横穴式石室]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 4]]

The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; revision.text:
{{Ancient burial mounds of Japan | Name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | Image = [[File:Sasazuka_Tumulus_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | Image caption = Burial Mound and Stone Chamber Entrance | Affiliation = {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}} | Location = {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}} | Latitude = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | Longitude = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | ISO = JP-42 | Shape = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Size = Approximately 66 m in diameter | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} inside) | Excavated Items = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | Construction Period = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Person Buried = | Historic Site = Included in the nationally designated {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible Cultural Property = Excavated items (national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}) | Special Notes = | Map = Japan Nagasaki | Icon = Round Kofun | Label = Sasazuka Kofun | Label Position = bottom }} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is an {{ill|ancient burial mound (kofun)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Iki City|ja|壱岐市}}, Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, {{ill|Nagasaki Prefecture|ja|長崎県}}. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} and one of the burial mounds that form part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群}}, designated as a national {{ill|Historic Site|ja|史跡|1=Historic site|lt=Historic site of Japan}}}. The excavated items are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the burial mound and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the former Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefecture's Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Bird's-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == Excavated Items == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable artifacts have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]], {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...eter: 54 meters). ...墳}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|[[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]], {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]], <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun| ...[[Category:nowiki former closing]] 2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|'''[[Category:nowiki former closing]]掛木古墳'''[[Category:nowiki former closing]]}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ([[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''壱岐市''')}}</nowiki>[[Category:nowiki former closing]]. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 ('''壱岐市''')}}. Designated on ... ...[[Category:nowiki former closing]] 818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Group]] | location = {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|[[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six burial mounds located in {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|[[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''壱岐市'''}}, Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is designated as a National {{ill|Histori ...[[Category:nowiki former closing]] 4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|[[Category:nowiki former closing]]'''笹塚古墳''' ('''壱岐市''')}} '''[[Category:nowiki former closing]]Sasazuka Kofun'''[[Category:nowiki former closing]] ('''笹塚古墳''') is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}[[Category:nowiki former closing]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|'''兵瀬古墳'''}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)}} * {{ill|Iki, Nagasaki|ja|壱岐市}} {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} '''Sasazuka Kofun''' (笹塚古墳) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[en:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[Category:円墳]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:円墳]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 4]]

Additional items include Sue ware, Haji ware bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron artifacts, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These artifacts are designated as national {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}} under the name "Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property (National Designation) === * Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National Cultural Property Database|201|00011271|Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 Excavated Items from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. * 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting * 1 Silver Item * 39 Gilded Items * 4 Bronze Items * 52 Iron Items * 46 Glass Items * 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses artifacts excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Cultural Heritage of Nagasaki Prefecture" [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Kofun in Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Nagasaki Prefecture]] [[Category:National Historic Sites of Japan in Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki&[[Category:nowiki former closing]]gt;{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers;
