(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; revision.comment: Merge [[Category:壱岐市の歴史]] into [[Category:History of Iki City]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a burial and burial goods parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | image = [[File:Sasazuka_Kofun_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Approximately 66 m in diameter | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... ...818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six Kofuns located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|qq=}} Sasazuka Kofun(笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827849|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]}} [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Group]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} [[ja:笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)]]

(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... ...818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six Kofuns located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|qq=}} Sasazuka Kofun(笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827849|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]}} [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[ja:%E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} [[ja:笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a burial and burial goods parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | image = [[File:Sasazuka_Kofun_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Approximately 66 m in diameter | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | image = [[File:Sasazuka_Kofun_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Approximately 66 m in diameter | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... ...818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six Kofuns located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|qq=}} Sasazuka Kofun(笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827849|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]}} [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[sn:E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} [[ja:笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q126604731]] Wikidata name: Sasazuka Kofun (Iki) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a burial and burial goods parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox ancient site | name = Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) | image = [[File:Sasazuka_Kofun_in_Katsumoto,_Iki,_Nagasaki.jpg|280px]] | caption = Kofun and Stone Chamber Entrance | part_of = {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}} | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure 936, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]] | coordinates = 33 | Lat_minutes = 48 | Lat_seconds = 10.25 | coordinates = 129 | Long_minutes = 42 | Long_seconds = 14.75 | omitted = JP-42 | type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Approximately 66 m in diameter | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}
(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. Designated on ... ...818 bytes (84 words) - 06:25, 19 January 2025 * [[Iki Kofun Cluster|Iki Kofun Group]] | location = [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture ...}}) is a collection of six Kofuns located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]], Nagasaki Prefecture. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|qq=}} Sasazuka Kofun(笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827849|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]}} [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[sn:E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} [[ja:笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

(with composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} inside) | burial goods = Gilded turtle-shaped decorative fittings, silver items, gilded items, and many other burial goods | Associated Mounds = | built = Late 6th century to the end of the 6th century | Interred_individual = | Historic site = Included in the nationally Historic site [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]} "Iki Kofun Group" | Tangible designation1 = excavations (national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]) | notes = | map = Japan Nagasaki | type = Round Kofun | map_dot_label = Sasazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is an [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki City]], Katsumoto Town, Yurihatafure, [[:en:Nagasaki Prefecture|Nagasaki Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and one of the Kofuns that type part of the {{ill|Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Cluster|lt=Iki Kofun Group|ja|壱岐古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Kofun Group|qq=}}, Historic site as a national [[:en:Historic site|Historic site of Japan]]}. The burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Overview == This kofun was built on a hill in the central part of Iki Island. Surveys of the Kofun and stone chamber were conducted by the Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education in 1989 (Heisei 1) and by the typeer Katsumoto Town Board of Education in 1990 (Heisei 2), with further investigations carried out between 2002 and 2003 (Heisei 14–15){{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/jiten/index.php/view/238 Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefectural Board of Education, "Encyclopedia of Nagasaki Prefectures Ruins").. The mound is circular, constructed in two tiers, with a diameter of approximately 66 meters. It is estimated to have been built in the late 6th century, with additional burials continuing until around the late 7th century{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial == [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Birds-eye View}}]] [[File:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber Expanded View}}]] The burial facility is a multi-chambered {{ill|horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, consisting of a main chamber, middle chamber, antechamber, and a passage, opening to the southwest{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. The stone chamber was already open during the Edo period, and records in the "{{ill|Iki Fudoki|ja|壱岐国風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}" mention inscriptions within the chamber, though these inscriptions were lost by the early Shōwa period{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 14.8 m{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}} (15.2 m) * Main chamber: Length 3.8 m, Width 2.7 m, Height 3.5 m * Middle chamber: Length 4.5 m, Width 2 m, Height 1.8 m * Antechamber: Length 4.2 m, Width 1.8 m, Height 1.8 m * Passage: Length 2.3 m A composite {{ill|box-shaped stone coffin|ja|箱式石棺|1=Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/box-shaped stone coffin|13=User:Immanuelle/Box-shaped stone coffin|qq=}} made of tuff is placed at the innermost part of the main chamber, measuring 2 m long, 1 m wide, and 0.7 m deep{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. It is said that another stone coffin once existed in front of it, though no trace of it has been found{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. == burial goods == Despite being looted in ancient times, notable burial goods have been discovered, including a unique turtle-shaped decorative fitting—the only one of its kind found in Japan—as well as gilded horse trappings, iron aprons, and other items reflecting advanced craftsmanship{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. Additional items include Sue pottery, Haji pottery bowls, Korean Peninsula-style ceramic vessels, iron burial goods, glass beads, gold rings, and spoon-shaped bronze items{{Sfn|Sasazuka Kofun (Zoku Kofun)|2002}}. These burial goods are Historic site as national [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] under the name "burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture". == Historic site == === Important designation1 (National designation1) === * burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture (Archaeological Materials) – Designated on June 8, 2007 (Heisei 19){{National designation1 Database|201|00011271|burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun, Nagasaki Prefecture}}[http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/482 burial goods from Sasazuka Kofun] (Nagasaki Prefecture Website).. ** 1 Gilded Turtle-shaped Decorative Fitting ** 1 Silver Item ** 39 Gilded Items ** 4 Bronze Items ** 52 Iron Items ** 46 Glass Items ** 19 Earthenware Items == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Iki City Ikikoku Museum|ja|壱岐市立一支国博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iki City Ikikoku Museum|qq=}} (Ashibe Town, Fukaetsurugamefure, Iki City) – Houses burial goods excavated from Sasazuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} * [http://www.pref.nagasaki.jp/bunkadb/index.php/view/645 Iki Kofun Group] - Nagasaki Prefecture Website "Historic site of Nagasaki Prefecture" of Nagasaki Prefecture]] * [[Hyoze Kofun]] ...nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...eter: 54 meters). ...墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>, {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}}, <nowiki>{{ill|Kakegi Kofun|...2 KB (196 words) - 06:26, 19 January 2025 * [[Kakegi Kofun]] ...ja|掛木古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakegi Kofun|qq=}}, and <nowiki>{{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}</nowiki>. Designated on ... ...|Kakegi Kofun|ja|掛木古墳}}, and {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}}. The group is Historic site as a National {{ill|Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Histori ...4 KB (487 words) - 06:01, 19 January 2025 * [[Sasazuka Kofun]] {{Otheruses|qq=}} Sasazuka Kofun(笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... == 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 == * {{ill|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|lt=Sasazuka Kofun|lt_2=(Iki City)Oni-no-Iw ...ers; east-west: 18 meters). ...City)|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * {{ill|Hyoze Kofun|ja|兵瀬古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyoze Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|ja|鬼の窟古墳 (壱岐市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Iwaya Kofun (Iki City)|qq=}} * [[:en:Iki, Nagasaki|Iki, Nagasaki]] {{Otheruses||Kofun in Iki City, Nagasaki Prefecture|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)}} Sasazuka Kofun (笹塚古墳) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in Higashiya-machi, {{i ... 6 KB (816 words) - 03:41, 18 January 2025 {{translated page|ja|笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)|version=100827849|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]][[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]]}} [[sn:Sasazuka Kofun (Iki City)]] [[sn:E7%AC%B9%E5%A1%9A%E5%8F%A4%E5%A2%B3 (%E5%A3%B1%E5%B2%90%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Iki Kofun Cluster]] [[Category:History of Iki City]] [[Category:National historic sites in Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Nagasaki prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Nagasaki prefecture]] {{Commons category|Sasazuka Kofun (Iki)}} [[ja:笹塚古墳 (壱岐市)]]
